title: The One with the Tea Leaves

jokes.text: Maybe he's in the sugar bowl. Joey? Nope.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if Joey is there, Monica and Chandler say they don't see him
jokes.punchline: Monica literally checks the sugar bowl for a full-grown man
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor with good economy - the visual of checking a tiny container for a person is genuinely funny

jokes.text: Well, at least you make each other laugh.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler are giggling at their bad lie about not seeing Joey
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dry observation about their obvious conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - Rachel calling out their obvious behavior with understated sarcasm

jokes.text: Ew, was Chandler naked?
jokes.setup: Monica says they were playing a game with a bagel on the floor
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately jumps to sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual misunderstanding humor - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Sort of like a ring-toss kind of situation?
jokes.setup: Previous naked game implication
jokes.punchline: Rachel gets more specific about the sexual scenario she's imagining
jokes.explanation: Escalates the sexual misunderstanding but relies on innuendo without much wit

jokes.text: Dude, I don't know. That's a pretty small hole.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions the 'naked bagel game' again
jokes.punchline: Chandler unwittingly continues the sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good callback timing but the humor is still just innuendo

jokes.text: Up until I was 25, I thought the only response to 'I love you' was 'Oh, crap.'
jokes.setup: Joey complains about rejection, Monica says she doesn't know what that's like
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals her hilariously specific romantic inexperience
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific joke that perfectly captures Monica's past neuroses with specific, quotable dialogue

jokes.text: I got shot down at fat camp.
jokes.setup: Previous rejection discussion
jokes.punchline: Monica adds another layer of personal embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Great character integration - perfectly fits Monica's backstory with efficient delivery

jokes.text: Boy, kids are mean when they're hungry.
jokes.setup: Monica's fat camp rejection story
jokes.punchline: Understated observation about the cruelty of hungry children
jokes.explanation: Nice understated capper to the fat camp story - good observational humor

jokes.text: And also, tea tends to give me the trots.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explains why she stopped reading tea leaves despite being accurate
jokes.punchline: Abrupt shift from mystical to crude physical reality
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - mixing spiritual and crude in character-appropriate way

jokes.text: Which can mean either a promotion or a violent death.
jokes.setup: Phoebe sees a ladder in Monica's tea leaves
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely extreme interpretation range
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - the extreme range from mundane to death is perfectly Phoebe

jokes.text: I'm the head chef. I can't get promoted.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's fortune of promotion or death
jokes.punchline: Monica's logical response implies she's getting the death option
jokes.explanation: Good logical follow-up that makes the death implication funnier through Monica's matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: You mean your pink shirt? / Faded salmon color? / No, I haven't seen your pink shirt.
jokes.setup: Ross describes his shirt as 'faded salmon color'
jokes.punchline: Joey cuts through the euphemism and repeats 'pink' to emphasize Ross's denial
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Ross's vanity and Joey's directness about the obvious

jokes.text: I'm sure you can get another one at Ann Taylor's.
jokes.setup: Ross is upset about losing his shirt
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests a women's clothing store
jokes.explanation: Continues the pink shirt theme with Joey's oblivious suggestion of women's store

jokes.text: Jump up my own ass and die.
jokes.setup: Ross explains Mona asked him to do one thing when they broke up
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude and hostile breakup demand
jokes.explanation: Shocking and quotable - the extreme vulgarity delivered matter-of-factly is effective

jokes.text: Mine is a picture of the Village People. What does that mean?
jokes.setup: Everyone else got mystical tea leaf readings
jokes.punchline: Ross somehow has a literal picture in his tea
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment - the specificity of 'Village People' makes it funnier than generic pop culture

jokes.text: Tea gives Phoebe the trots.
jokes.setup: Awkward silence between Rachel and Joey
jokes.punchline: Monica fills dead air with the most inappropriate callback possible
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed callback that breaks tension in the worst possible way - excellent awkward humor

jokes.text: I'm not great at the advice. Can I interest you in a sarcastic comment?
jokes.setup: Rachel asks how Chandler knows things will get better
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits his limitations and offers his specialty instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-defining joke that's self-aware about Chandler's role in the show - perfectly quotable

jokes.text: Some cheese?
jokes.setup: Chandler offers sarcastic comment, then
jokes.punchline: Randomly offers cheese as if it's another service he provides
jokes.explanation: Perfect non-sequitur delivered with same helpful tone - economy and timing make it work

jokes.text: Two hundred CDs, not one of them in the right case.
jokes.setup: Monica discovers CDs in wrong cases
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals the full scope of his chaotic system
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke showing Chandler's complete disregard for Monica's organizational needs

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you guys have such problems. I feel so terrible for you.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler panic over CD organization
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic perspective check on their trivial problems
jokes.explanation: Good reality check humor but fairly standard sitcom beat

jokes.text: They were just giving those away at the store. / In exchange for money.
jokes.setup: Chandler defends having Miami Vice soundtrack saying they gave it away
jokes.punchline: Monica's deadpan clarification of what 'giving away' meant
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with Monica's typical precision correcting Chandler's logic

jokes.text: My boss wants to buy my baby.
jokes.setup: Rachel needs to invent a work problem to talk to Joey
jokes.punchline: She blurts out the most absurd work scenario possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd premise that drives the entire subplot - shocking and quotable

jokes.text: Yeah, that would have been a much simpler problem.
jokes.setup: Joey suggests it was about maternity leave
jokes.punchline: Rachel's understated acknowledgment of her terrible lie
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to her own absurd story choice

jokes.text: Jim-Jim Nelson. I'm Phoebe-Phoebe Buffay.
jokes.setup: Jim introduces himself with repeated first name
jokes.punchline: Phoebe mirrors his awkward introduction pattern
jokes.explanation: Mild awkward humor showing Phoebe's tendency to mirror odd behavior

jokes.text: [MOUTHS] It's not pink.
jokes.setup: Man says the shirt is 'awfully pink'
jokes.punchline: Ross mouths denial while hiding behind the door
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback showing Ross's continued denial even while hiding

jokes.text: Honey, both yours.
jokes.setup: Chandler says they both have Annie soundtrack so they're meant to be
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of Chandler's romantic moment

jokes.text: You think you can just buy my friend's baby?
jokes.setup: Rachel's fake story about boss wanting to buy her baby
jokes.punchline: Joey confronts the 'baby buyer' at Ralph Lauren
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Joey taking action on the fake story - perfectly in character

jokes.text: Do you like to party?
jokes.setup: Phoebe starts normal conversation about being a masseuse
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately shifts to creepy pickup line
jokes.explanation: Standard creepy guy behavior - predictable but serves the cringe storyline

jokes.text: I write erotic novels... for children.
jokes.setup: Phoebe gives Jim another chance to be normal
jokes.punchline: He reveals the most disturbing career possible
jokes.explanation: Shocking and perfectly structured dark humor - the pause makes it even more disturbing and quotable

jokes.text: They're wildly unpopular.
jokes.setup: The disturbing career revelation
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual follow-up as if that's the only problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up that makes the initial joke even darker through understatement

jokes.text: Yep. A pretty huge...
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks if he really has a Ph.D.
jokes.punchline: Jim starts to make it sexual before being cut off
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual misdirection that feels cheap after the stronger material

jokes.text: You could always sell your baby.
jokes.setup: Rachel panics about losing her job over the fake story
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests going along with the original fake premise
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Joey's simple logic - if the lie caused problems, make it true

jokes.text: Chandler has two copies of Annie.
jokes.setup: Argument about who messed up what
jokes.punchline: Monica deflects with random organizational discovery
jokes.explanation: Mild callback showing Monica's OCD priorities even during arguments

jokes.text: Not touching myself, if that makes anyone less uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Ross caught hiding in Mona's apartment
jokes.punchline: Ross's attempt to clarify what he wasn't doing there
jokes.explanation: Good awkward humor as Ross makes the situation worse by over-explaining

jokes.text: I certainly did not mean that I felt that I was due your baby.
jokes.setup: Boss apologizing for due date comment
jokes.punchline: Accidental wordplay on 'due' that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows how the situation spiraled from innocent comment

jokes.text: A new grocery store. / What? / A new grocery store.
jokes.setup: Phoebe lists places she needs to avoid because of Jim
jokes.punchline: She accidentally includes 'adult bookstore' and tries to cover
jokes.explanation: Standard 'cover up the inappropriate thing' humor but well-executed timing

jokes.text: This is like Disneyland for me.
jokes.setup: Monica enters the dry cleaner
jokes.punchline: Her extreme enthusiasm for stain removal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character joke - her cleaning obsession makes mundane places magical

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, well, I'm sorry about that too. But what are you gonna do?
jokes.setup: New guy compliments her beauty
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual acceptance of being too beautiful
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe moment - treating her beauty like an unavoidable burden with perfect nonchalance

jokes.text: So long as I understand that the money should not be construed as a down payment on this or any other child that I may bear.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains the resolution to the baby-buying incident
jokes.punchline: The legal department's overly specific disclaimer about not buying babies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how corporate panic led to absurdly specific legal language

jokes.text: My gynecologist tried to kill me.
jokes.setup: Awkward moment between Rachel and Joey returns
jokes.punchline: Rachel blurts out another random 'problem' to fill silence
jokes.explanation: Great callback to her tendency to invent absurd problems when nervous

tmdb_overview: Phoebe's tea leaf reading predicts meeting her dream man. Joey and Rachel's friendship becomes awkward after his confession.
